rawCommits.subject: style(update-banner): use black background for ready variant
rawCommits.body: Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: style(update-banner): use black background for ready variant

rawCommits.subject: fix(skills): block delete while installed elsewhere and hide stale catalog entries
rawCommits.body:
The "Delete skill" flow in the agent sidekick had two gaps that let
state drift between agents:

1. Deleting a user-authored skill from one agent removed the SKILL.md
   file on disk but left every other agent's installation record
   pointing at a now-missing file. The UI showed the cascade warning
   but only uninstalled from the current agent before firing the
   delete, so CEO would still list the skill as installed after
   Agent02 deleted it.
2. The harness catalog (GET api/skills) is in-memory and only
   reconciles on rescan, so a just-deleted skill kept reappearing
   under "Available" until the harness caught up.

Server-side delete_my_skill now enumerates local agents, asks the
harness which skills each one has installed, and refuses with 409
when the skill is still live anywhere -- returning the offending
{agent_id, name} list so the UI can tell the user which agents to
clean up first. The list_skills proxy drops any catalog entry whose
~/.aura/skills/<name>/SKILL.md is gone, so the filesystem stays the
source of truth for what shows under "Available".

The SkillsTab delete flow drops the pre-emptive current-agent
uninstall (it was masking the real state) and renders the 409
blocker list inline in the confirm modal with the Delete button
disabled until every blocking agent has uninstalled.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: block delete while installed elsewhere and hide stale catalog entries

rawCommits.subject: refactor(agent-tools): unify tool registry with Surface-declared session inclusion
rawCommits.body:
Replace the fragmented "tier-1 / tier-2" + CEO-preset short-circuit model
with a single capability-aware registry filtered at session-open time.

Every tool now declares `fn surface() -> Surface` (`Always` or
`OnDemand`), and `AgentPermissions::ceo_preset()` carries the new
`Capability::ReadAllProjects` / `WriteAllProjects` wildcards so CEO
and non-CEO bundles flow through exactly the same
`permissions_satisfy_requirements` + `build_session_tool_names`
pipeline. This fixes the reported CEO bug where `list_agents`,
`send_to_agent`, and `list_agent_instances` were missing from the
live session: `list_agent_instances` is now `Surface::Always` and
its `ReadProjectFromArg` requirement is satisfied via
`ReadAllProjects`.

Also invalidate the agent's direct chat session and the dispatcher
`permissions_cache` entry in `update_agent`, so toggling capabilities
in the UI takes effect on the next chat turn without a server restart.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: unify tool registry with Surface-declared session inclusion

rawCommits.subject: feat(settings): add upgrade control under version
rawCommits.body:
Add an update status block beneath the Settings 'Version' row so desktop
users can see whether they are on the latest version, check for updates,
install available updates, and observe download/install/failure states.
Falls back to a 'managed by the server' message on the web where the
native updater is unavailable.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: add upgrade control under version

rawCommits.subject: fix(permissions): invalidate every session owned by an agent on caps change
rawCommits.body:
The previous invalidation only dropped the `agent:{id}` direct-chat
session. Regular agents in a project context actually chat through
`instance:{agent_instance_id}` sessions, whose stale `installed_tools`
list survived the permissions update and caused the reported
"I enabled ReadAgent but `list_agents` still isn't available" bug.

Tag each `ChatSession` with its owning agent id at open time
(populated from `SessionConfig::agent_id`, which already carries the
canonical agent id for both direct and instance flows) so
`update_agent` can sweep the registry and drop every session owned by
the agent whose capabilities just changed — direct and instance alike.
The next chat turn cold-starts and re-seeds `installed_tools` through
the unified capability-aware `build_session_tools` filter.

Also restyle the capability toggles in `PermissionsTab` with a bordered
black track and high-contrast knob so the on/off state is visible
against the dark panel background.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: invalidate every session owned by an agent on caps change

rawCommits.subject: fix(permissions): read fresh parent caps per instance chat turn, guard PUT echo
rawCommits.body:
Two gaps kept regular-agent capability toggles from taking effect
after Save:

1. Project-instance chats used `instance.permissions`, a snapshot
   captured when the instance was first bound to its project. Toggling
   a capability on the agent template updated `Agent.permissions` but
   never touched the instance snapshot, so every project-bound chat
   kept serving the stale tool list forever. The snapshot was always
   documented as a parent-lookup-failed fallback — make that explicit
   by reading `state.agent_service.get_agent_async` at cold-start and
   falling back to the stored snapshot only if the lookup fails.

2. `update_agent` trusted `agent_from_network(&net_agent).permissions`
   for the PUT response's permissions projection. When aura-network
   accepts the write but omits `permissions` from its response body
   (or an older deployment drops the field on serialize), the response
   deserializes to `AgentPermissions::default()` and `patchAgent` in
   the UI wipes every toggle the user just saved. After a successful
   PUT, compare the response's permissions to what we submitted and —
   on mismatch — trust the request-side value while logging an
   upstream regression warning.

Together this makes "toggle ReadAgent on → Save → send message →
`list_agents` is available" work for regular agents in project
contexts without any backend changes.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: read fresh parent caps per instance chat turn, guard PUT echo

rawCommits.subject: refactor(update-control): share UpdateControl across settings surfaces
rawCommits.body:
Move the update status/upgrade control into components/UpdateControl so
it can be rendered from both the standalone Settings view and the Team
Settings > General panel, which is the one most users actually see. This
adds the 'latest version'/upgrade UI next to the existing Version row in
the Team Settings About group.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: share UpdateControl across settings surfaces

rawCommits.subject: perf(chat): fan out per-session event reads and bound by target window
rawCommits.body:
Replace the sequential per-session list_events loop in
load_latest_agent_events_from_storage_result and
load_project_session_history with a shared helper that walks sessions
newest-first, fetches events in parallel batches of four, and stops as
soon as the caller's limit+offset window is satisfied. Historical chat
opens with many past sessions no longer pay O(total lifetime events) of
storage reads on every open.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: perf(chat): fan out per-session event reads and bound by target window

rawCommits.subject: style(org-settings): move Updates row to the end of the About group
rawCommits.body:
Reorder the About group to Version / Commit / Built / Updates so the
interactive upgrade control sits at the bottom of the static build
metadata instead of splitting the Version row from Commit and Built.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: style(org-settings): move Updates row to the end of the About group

rawCommits.subject: style(update-control): wrap and right-align the server-managed message
rawCommits.body:
Give the unsupported-browser message its own class that caps the width,
right-aligns the text, and removes the unused top margin so it reads as
two balanced lines at the end of the row instead of a single long line.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: style(update-control): wrap and right-align the server-managed message

rawCommits.subject: perf(chat): cache project-agent discovery with a short TTL
rawCommits.body:
Wrap `find_matching_project_agents` with a 30s in-memory cache keyed by
(jwt, agent_id). The orgs -> projects -> project_agents fan-out
underneath it was the dominant fixed cost on every chat open and every
chat turn, and since bindings change only on explicit create/delete
paths a <=30s stale result is safe. On cold boot the active agent's
history fetch populates the cache, and the sidebar preview prefetches
for other agents then hit it instead of re-walking the same
orgs/projects tree.

Invalidate on the lazy Home-project auto-bind path so the re-read after
`ensure_agent_home_project_and_binding` sees the new binding rather
than the empty snapshot the first call populated.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: perf(chat): cache project-agent discovery with a short TTL

rawCommits.subject: perf(agents): deprioritize sidebar preview prefetch on cold boot
rawCommits.body:
The agents sidebar was fetching a 80-event history preview for every
other agent as soon as the list loaded, with concurrency 4. On cold
boot this collided with the foreground chat's own history fetch: the
active agent's request was queued behind N parallel previews and
raced against them for backend capacity, pushing first-paint out.

Three tweaks, no behavior change once everything settles:

- Exclude the currently selected agent from the prefetch queue. The
  active chat view is already fetching that one; running it here just
  puts the foreground request at the back of the line.
- Wait for the active chat's history to resolve before kicking off
  previews, so the foreground round-trip has an uncontested path.
- Drop concurrency from 4 to 2 and schedule via `requestIdleCallback`
  so the previews fill in during browser idle time rather than
  competing with initial render work.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: perf(agents): deprioritize sidebar preview prefetch on cold boot

rawCommits.subject: perf(chat): persist chat history to IndexedDB for instant paint on reload
rawCommits.body:
`useChatHistoryStore` was memory-only, so every browser reload
re-started every chat view at status: "loading" and flashed a spinner
until the server round-trip resolved. Add a `chatHistory` object store
to the shared `aura-browser-store` IDB wrapper and:

- Write the final display-event list there whenever `fetchHistory`
  succeeds.
- Expose `hydrateFromCache(key)` that populates the in-memory entry
  from IDB when the caller mounts with no entry yet. Uses the
  persisted timestamp (not `fetchedAt: 0`) so `useChatHistorySync`'s
  stale-guard doesn't treat it as loading and the view paints
  immediately while the network revalidation runs in parallel.
- Call `hydrateFromCache` from `useChatHistorySync` alongside
  `fetchHistory`, and clear the cached entry on explicit
  `clearHistory`.

IDB failures fall back to the existing localStorage shim via
`browserDbSet`/`browserDbGet`, so the feature degrades gracefully in
private browsing contexts where IDB is blocked.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: perf(chat): persist chat history to IndexedDB for instant paint on reload

rawCommits.subject: perf(agents): drop the /agents index lazy-load + agent-list gate
rawCommits.body:
Two cold-boot waterfall steps on first navigation to `/agents`:

1. `AgentChatView` was `React.lazy`-imported from the agents route
   table, adding a dynamic import round-trip to the critical path.
   The projects app already imports `AgentChatView` eagerly (see
   `apps/projects/routes.tsx`), so splitting it here saved zero bytes
   in the initial bundle — just cost a chunk fetch. Import eagerly.

2. `AgentIndexRedirect` waited for `fetchAgents` to resolve before
   deciding where to go, even when `localStorage.lastAgentId` already
   told us exactly which agent the user wants. Desktop now redirects
   synchronously off the cached id; `AgentChatView` will handle any
   stale id via its existing missing-agent flow, so no correctness
   regression. Mobile still shows the library prompt.

Combined, first meaningful paint on `/agents` no longer blocks on
either the chunk fetch or the agents-list network round-trip.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: perf(agents): drop the /agents index lazy-load + agent-list gate

rawCommits.subject: fix(permissions): preserve shadow bundle when aura-network drops the permissions column on read
rawCommits.body:
The PUT path already had defensive reconciliation that trusts the
caller's submitted bundle when aura-network fails to echo it back,
but the GET path blindly overwrote the local shadow with whatever
`permissions` column the network response carried. For non-CEO
library agents that column comes back empty, so every `GET /agents`
on app boot clobbered the toggles the user had saved — capabilities
appeared to stick in the current session and then silently turn off
on the next restart. CEO agents were immune only because
`normalized_for_identity` re-expands their bundle to the canonical
preset after the network hydrate.

Adds a symmetric read-side reconciliation helper on `AgentService`
and wires it into every path that turns a `NetworkAgent` into an
`Agent` before saving the shadow: `list_agents`, `get_agent`,
`hydrate_shadow_from_network`, `get_agent_async`,
`get_agent_with_jwt`, and `installed_tools::load_agent`. Also aligns
`SessionConfig.agent_permissions` with the already-normalized
bundle `installed_tools` is built from so the harness dispatcher's
capability checks can't disagree with the surfaced toolset.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: preserve shadow bundle when aura-network drops the permissions column on read

rawCommits.subject: feat(permissions): autosave capability toggles on change, replace Save/Discard bar with status pill
rawCommits.body:
Toggling a capability, scope chip, or project-access entry in the
Permissions sidekick tab now persists automatically ~350ms after
the last edit — no more Save/Discard bar to remember. Rapid flicker
coalesces into a single PUT via a debounce timer, and if the tab
unmounts or the user switches agents with a pending debounce, the
latest draft is flushed synchronously on teardown so nothing gets
silently dropped.

A small `AutosaveStatus` pill in the Scope section header echoes
the save lifecycle: spinner + "Saving…" while in flight, a brief
"Saved" flash on success, and a red "Save failed" label with an
inline Retry action on error. Keyed the prop-sync effect on
`agent_id` only (not `agent.permissions`) so the `patchAgent`
round-trip after our own successful save can't race against
in-flight keystrokes and snap the draft back to a stale snapshot.

Also fills in the `readAllProjects` / `writeAllProjects` entries
in `CAPABILITY_LABELS` that had fallen out of sync with the wire
type (the build started failing once the test suite referenced
them via the corrected CEO fixture).

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: autosave capability toggles on change, replace Save/Discard bar with status pill

rawCommits.subject: ci(android): fail fast on missing fastlane release secrets
rawCommits.body:
The `ship-android` job was dying deep inside `fastlane android release` with
`Missing required environment variable ANDROID_PLAY_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON_BASE64`,
and only ever surfaced the first missing var at a time. Add a preflight that
checks every required secret for the selected lane in one pass and prints an
actionable error before the fastlane step runs. Wire it into both
android-mobile.yml (ship-android) and release-mobile-nightly.yml (build-android,
github_release lane) so signed-build and Play Store paths both fail loudly and
early when a secret is unset.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: fail fast on missing fastlane release secrets

rawCommits.subject: feat(notes): sidekick TOC first, scroll-to-heading, split Created at/by rows
rawCommits.body:
Reorders the Notes sidekick tabs to Table of contents, Info, Comments
and moves the TOC into its own panel. Clicking a TOC entry now scrolls
the editor to the N-th heading via a data attribute on the editor root.
The Info panel splits the old "Created . author" row into separate
"Created at" (date only) and "Created by" rows, with a UUID fallback
that resolves the current user's legacy user_id to their display name.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: feat(notes-server): notes follow project Local workspace path; record display_name
rawCommits.body:
resolve_notes_root now prefers a project's `local_workspace_path` when
configured, storing notes at `<workspace>/notes/`. Switching the
workspace path triggers a one-time migration of any previously
slug-bound folder under `<data_dir>/notes/` into the new location,
preserving subfolders and comment sidecars; the legacy folder's
`.project-id` marker is cleared so it no longer claims the project.

New notes now record the author's display_name in the `created_by`
frontmatter field (with a user_id fallback) so the Info sidekick can
render the real name without a separate lookup.

Adds unit tests for move_notes_contents (including the
don't-clobber-existing-destination guard), copy_recursive, and the
new workspace-path branch of resolve_notes_root.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: notes follow project Local workspace path; record display_name

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): use app-black bg for sent doc/long-text preview widgets
rawCommits.body:
User messages that wrap only a FileAttachmentBlock or LargeTextBlock now
drop the grey chat-bubble chrome and let the widget's own dark card read
as a first-class element on the app background, matching the rest of
the UI instead of sitting on --color-bg-secondary.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: use app-black bg for sent doc/long-text preview widgets

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): allow chat input to scroll internally when at max-height
rawCommits.body:
The bottom chat composer auto-resizes up to min(70vh, 800px) and then
pins height at the cap. The textarea had overflow: hidden, so once the
content passed the cap the top lines became unreachable. Switch to
overflow-y: auto (keeping overflow-x: hidden) so the native scrollbar
takes over and users can scroll to the top of long pasted/typed text.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: allow chat input to scroll internally when at max-height

rawCommits.subject: feat(left-menu): share project drag-reorder across Tasks, Notes, Process
rawCommits.body:
Extracts the Projects-app root-group drag-and-drop wiring into a shared
`useLeftMenuProjectReorder` hook in `features/left-menu` and adopts it in
Tasks, Notes, and Process so every app that renders `LeftMenuTree` with
project groups gets the same pointer-based reorder behavior, excluded
entries (Archived root, section variants), search-off and min-two gating,
and global persistence via `useProjectsListStore.saveProjectOrder`.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: share project drag-reorder across Tasks, Notes, Process

rawCommits.subject: feat(chat): remember last model per agent across restarts
rawCommits.body:
Model persistence previously used a single `aura-selected-model:<adapterType>`
localStorage key, so every agent on the same adapter shared one setting
and switching agents (or closing the app) lost per-agent preferences.

Add an agent-scoped key (`aura-selected-model:agent:<agentId>`) that takes
precedence when present, while keeping the adapter key in sync as a
fallback for agents with no saved preference and for legacy readers.
Thread `agentId` through `chat-ui-store.init`, `setSelectedModel`, and
`syncAvailableModels`, and wire it up from `useChatPanelState` and the
model picker in `ChatInputBar`. Covered by new `models.test.ts` cases
and an additional assertion in `chat-ui-store.test.ts`.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: remember last model per agent across restarts

rawCommits.subject: feat(files): show current directory breadcrumb and auto-refresh tree
rawCommits.body:
The sidekick Files view now renders a small path header above the tree
showing the workspace directory the tree is rooted at, with long paths
elided to the last few segments and the full path available on hover.

Also add a visibility-gated 3s polling refresh in useFileExplorerState
so external file changes (e.g. agent edits, tool runs) surface without
waiting for a turn boundary or a FileOpsApplied event. The existing
debounce and event-driven refreshes continue to coalesce overlapping
updates, so the polling cost is bounded.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: show current directory breadcrumb and auto-refresh tree

rawCommits.subject: feat(sidekick): push optimistic specs/tasks at ToolUseStart
rawCommits.body:
Previously `pushPendingSpec` / `pushPendingTask` only fired on the
`ToolCall` event, which is emitted after the model has finished
streaming the tool arguments. On multi-second tool calls the sidekick
list sat empty until the call completed, defeating the point of the
pending placeholder.

Fire the optimistic push on `ToolUseStart` / `ToolCallStarted` instead
(with "Generating spec…" / "Creating task…" placeholders), and update
the title from `ToolCallSnapshot` as the model streams `title`,
`spec_id`, `description` fields in. The existing `ToolCall` handler
is retained as a final fallback and is idempotent via id, so network
gaps don't leave the sidekick stuck without a placeholder.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: push optimistic specs/tasks at ToolUseStart

rawCommits.subject: fix(spec-tools): stop double-shipping markdown and serialize multi-spec turns
rawCommits.body:
Long single-turn spec generation was blowing through the Anthropic proxy's
output TPM bucket because the CEO prompt asked the agent to (a) stream the
full spec markdown as visible assistant text AND (b) pass that same
markdown back in `create_spec`/`update_spec` -- roughly doubling output
tokens per spec. With 5+ sub-specs in one turn this reliably tripped
"Too many requests. Retry after 7 seconds." from the router.

  * Rewrite CEO behavioral guideline #6 to instruct the agent to pass the
    full markdown in `markdown_contents` only, keeping visible text to a
    short preview or table-of-contents. The tool already streams the body
    to the UI as it saves, so the duplicate is pure waste.
  * Add guideline #7 telling the agent to emit one `create_spec` call at
    a time for multi-spec requests instead of fan-out -- this keeps each
    tool output under the per-minute output-token ceiling and gives the
    user incremental progress.
  * Mirror the guidance into `create_spec` / `update_spec` tool
    descriptions and `markdown_contents` parameter docs so the model sees
    it even when guideline #6 is compacted out of context.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: stop double-shipping markdown and serialize multi-spec turns

rawCommits.subject: feat(context): update context usage live during streaming
rawCommits.body:
The bottom-bar Context pill only updated on `AssistantMessageEnd`, so
long turns left the indicator frozen for seconds or minutes while the
model was actively consuming context.

Project utilization from streaming token deltas instead:
- `context-usage-store` now caches a per-stream utilization-per-token
  ratio derived from the last authoritative `AssistantMessageEnd` and
  exposes `bumpEstimatedTokens(key, delta)` that updates the estimate
  and projects utilization monotonically (clamped to 1). The next
  authoritative value reconciles the optimistic one.
- `build-stream-handler` bumps estimated tokens on `TextDelta`,
  `ThinkingDelta`, and `ToolResult` using a cheap 1-token-per-4-chars
  approximation (`approxTokensFromText`).

Covered by new unit tests in `context-usage-store.test.ts`.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: update context usage live during streaming

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): dedupe duplicate thinking blocks on interleaved streams
rawCommits.body:
When a tool-call or other non-thinking event briefly landed between
ThinkingDelta events, handleThinkingDelta would push a second thinking
timeline item. Both items rendered the same global thinkingText buffer,
producing two identical thinking blocks in the UI (most visible at app
or agent startup).

- Track thinking text per timeline segment via an optional text field
  on TimelineItem, so each segment carries only its own deltas.
- In ActivityTimeline, merge contiguous thinking items into a single
  rendered ThinkingBlock and prefer the per-segment text, falling back
  to the legacy global thinkingText for historical messages.
- Add regression tests covering consecutive deltas collapsing into one
  segment and deltas split by a tool call producing two segments with
  independent text.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: dedupe duplicate thinking blocks on interleaved streams

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): move expanded input scrollbar to far right past send button
rawCommits.body:
Let .inputRow be the sole scroll container so the scrollbar renders on
the outer edge of the input (past the sticky send button) instead of
flush against the textarea's right side.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: move expanded input scrollbar to far right past send button

rawCommits.subject: fix(notes): unify scroll, retheme links and toggle, and pad title
rawCommits.body:
- Make Markdown mode share .scrollArea + OverlayScrollbar with Rich by
  auto-growing the textarea (height=scrollHeight, overflow hidden) and
  dropping the full-bleed .centerColumnMarkdown override, so both modes
  scroll through the same overlay thumb and layout.
- Increase the top padding of .scrollArea so the first-block title has
  breathing room under the mode toggle.
- Route the editor link color through var(--color-accent) / hover, so it
  follows the selected ZUI accent (real green #00e676 under data-accent
  'green') instead of the stray #22c55e fallback of the never-defined
  --color-link-accent token.
- The active Rich/Markdown tab now uses var(--color-text-primary) for
  both its label and underline, giving the selected tab pure white text
  and a white underline instead of the greyed var(--color-text) and the
  green border.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: unify scroll, retheme links and toggle, and pad title

rawCommits.subject: fix(preview): stop horizontal scrollbar on spec/file preview body
rawCommits.body:
Make overflow-x explicit on .previewBody and constrain markdown children so
wide code, tables, or unbreakable inline tokens scroll within their own
blocks instead of the whole preview panel.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: stop horizontal scrollbar on spec/file preview body

rawCommits.subject: fix(sidekick): match preview overlay header height to search input
rawCommits.body:
Align spec/task title bar in the sidekick preview overlay with the
panel search input by using --control-height-sm (32px) instead of 44px,
so the two stacked rows read as a single consistent strip.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: match preview overlay header height to search input

rawCommits.subject: fix(preview): wrap pre blocks instead of horizontal scrolling
rawCommits.body:
Switch .markdown pre from overflow-x: auto to white-space: pre-wrap with
break-word / overflow-wrap: anywhere so long code lines and pipe-delimited
table sources wrap to the panel width instead of producing a scrollbar.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: wrap pre blocks instead of horizontal scrolling

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): restore scroll-up in chat input, float send/attach over textarea
rawCommits.body:
Commit 5694c24c moved the scroll container from the textarea to the
outer .inputRow, which broke native caret-follow behavior: pressing
Arrow Up through a long message moved the caret off-screen because the
textarea itself had overflow: hidden and the ancestor did not follow
the caret.

Let the textarea own the scroll again with max-height: min(70vh, 800px)
and overflow-y: auto so the browser's native keyboard/wheel scrolling
works. Re-apply the same cap in autoResizeTextarea so the inline height
never exceeds the CSS max-height and the scrollbar actually engages.

Reposition the attach and send buttons as absolute overlays anchored to
the bottom-left and bottom-right corners of the input, with left/right
textarea padding so typed text never slides under them. The send button
sits just inside the right edge, leaving the native scrollbar visible
on the far right.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: restore scroll-up in chat input, float send/attach over textarea

rawCommits.subject: fix(sidekick): dedupe optimistic spec/task placeholders
rawCommits.body:
Pushing placeholders earlier (at ToolUseStart / ToolCallSnapshot) made a
few leak paths visible: a stream that aborted before ToolResult, a tool
result whose body wasn't parseable, or a SpecSaved that arrived via the
engine WebSocket out of order with the chat stream's FIFO-shift all left
`pending-*` rows in the sidekick, which then appeared alongside the real
spec with the same title (the triplicate 01/02/03 rows in the bug).

Make the placeholder lifecycle robust via three belt-and-suspenders
guards:

- pushSpec / pushTask in sidekick-store evict any existing `pending-*`
  entries whose title matches when a real (non-pending-id) record is
  pushed. Also rebinds the preview if it was viewing the evicted
  placeholder, so the user doesn't lose context when the real record
  arrives.
- pushPendingSpec / pushPendingTask skip the placeholder entirely if
  a real item with the same title is already in the store and this
  tool-call id is not already tracked, preventing duplicate flashes
  when the agent re-issues create_spec/create_task for an existing
  title.
- SpecSaved / TaskSaved in the chat-stream handler replace the fragile
  FIFO shift() with a title-matching drop (dropPendingByTitle), so
  concurrent specs in flight no longer evict the wrong placeholder.
- Any placeholders still tracked when the stream ends, errors, aborts,
  or hits Done are removed from the sidekick (clearAllPendingArtifacts)
  instead of leaking into the next turn.

Tests:
- sidekick-store.test.ts: pushSpec/pushTask title-eviction, preview
  rebind, and the no-op case where the incoming record itself is a
  pending id.
- optimistic-artifacts.test.ts (new): pushPendingSpec/Task skip behavior,
  idempotent ref tracking, removePendingArtifact, clearAllPendingArtifacts,
  and dropPendingByTitle.
- chat-stream-integration.test.ts mock extended with empty specs/tasks
  so the new sidekick lookups are exercised.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: dedupe optimistic spec/task placeholders

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): stop QuotaExceededError spam and unregister dead remote agents
rawCommits.body:
`browserDbSet` mirrored every successful IndexedDB write into `localStorage`
from inside the IDB `transaction.oncomplete` callback. During long spec
runs the serialized transcript easily exceeds the ~5 MB localStorage
quota, so `setItem` threw `QuotaExceededError` synchronously, skipping
`resolve()` and leaving the returned promise pending forever. Drop the
write-through for `chatHistory` (IDB is authoritative), wrap the remaining
fallback writes in try/catch so they can never escape an IDB callback,
and always settle the promise via a single `settle()` helper. Also purge
any legacy `aura-idb:chatHistory:*` mirrors on boot to free the budget
they already consumed.

`pollRemoteAgents` added every registered agent to a module-level `Set`
and never removed it, so once the swarm gateway forgot an agent the UI
polled `GET /api/agents/<id>/remote_agent/state` every 30 s forever and
the console filled with 404s. Unregister the agent on 400/404/410, clear
the shared interval once the poll set empties, and cover it with a test.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: stop QuotaExceededError spam and unregister dead remote agents

rawCommits.subject: fix(sidekick): keep taskbar bottom border visible on icon hover
rawCommits.body:
Render the border as a 1px overlay pseudo-element so ghost button hover
backgrounds no longer cover it.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: keep taskbar bottom border visible on icon hover

rawCommits.subject: fix(sidekick): drop deleted spec/task from layout cache to auto-update views
rawCommits.body:
Deleting a spec or task only updated the sidekick-store's in-memory
`deletedSpecIds`/`deletedTaskIds` filter, but the react-query
project-layout cache that feeds `initialSpecs`/`initialTasks` to every
view was left intact. Sidekick TaskList/SpecList derive their local
state from that cache, and sibling views (Kanban, mobile work/tasks)
read it directly with no filter, so the deleted item kept reappearing
until a page refresh forced a refetch.

- Add `removeSpecFromProjectLayout` / `removeTaskFromProjectLayout`
  helpers in `project-queries.ts` that strip an entry from the cached
  `ProjectLayoutBundle`.
- `useDeleteSpec`: after `api.deleteSpec` succeeds, patch the layout
  cache alongside the existing store `removeSpec` call.
- `TaskList` task delete: patch the layout cache optimistically
  alongside `removeTask`, matching the existing optimistic flow that
  rolls back via `pushTask` on API failure.

Now every view sourcing `initialSpecs`/`initialTasks` (Sidekick lists,
mobile work/tasks pages) reflects the delete immediately without a
refresh.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: drop deleted spec/task from layout cache to auto-update views

rawCommits.subject: fix(sidekick): keep preview overlay header border visible on X hover
rawCommits.body:
Paint the overlay header's bottom border via an ::after pseudo-element (same trick as the sidekick taskbar) so close/back button hover backgrounds no longer cover it.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: keep preview overlay header border visible on X hover

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): ensure per-agent model choice survives the cold-boot race
rawCommits.body:
`init` early-returned once any selectedModel was installed, so a first
pass that fired before `useAgentChatMeta` resolved (agentId known but
adapterType/defaultModel still undefined) could strand the picker on the
adapter default until the user picked again. Let `init` re-read the
per-agent localStorage value once `hasAgentScopedModel(agentId)` becomes
true, and have `syncAvailableModels` upgrade to that value when it
differs, even if the current selection is still valid for the adapter.

Also stop `loadPersistedModel` from leaking one agent's selection into
an untouched agent via the legacy adapter-scoped key (`persistModel`
updates it on every pick). When a caller supplies an `agentId` with no
agent-scoped entry, fall straight through to the adapter default; the
adapter-scoped fallback is kept only for legacy callers that have no
`agentId`.

Update the existing "falls back to adapter-scoped key" test to assert
the new no-leak contract, and cover the cold-boot race and the "don't
overwrite an active in-memory pick when the agent has no stored value"
case in chat-ui-store tests.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: ensure per-agent model choice survives the cold-boot race

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): keep trailing prose above tool cards during streaming
rawCommits.body:
When an LLM turn emits `text -> tool_call_started -> more text`, the
second text delta used to push a new text timeline item *after* the tool
items, so the trailing words flashed below the tool cards until the run
finished and history was refetched (at which point the persisted layout
put all prose above the tools and the UI "snapped" into place).

Fold the delta back into the most recent text item in the timeline
instead, inserting a blank-line break so pre- and post-tool paragraphs
stay visually distinct inside the merged markdown block. Stream buffer
and text-item content grow in lockstep so the word-by-word reveal still
maps correctly.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: keep trailing prose above tool cards during streaming

rawCommits.subject: fix(agents): preserve CEO preset when editing name or system prompt
rawCommits.body:
Editing the CEO SuperAgent's name (e.g. to "Orion") or system prompt
stripped the preset because the PUT handler never reconciled the
response's empty `permissions` bundle against the local shadow, and
the narrow CEO/CEO identity repair stopped matching after rename.

- `update_agent` now calls `reconcile_permissions_with_shadow` before
  `save_agent_shadow`, symmetric with the GET / list paths.
- `save_agent_shadow` / `save_agent_shadows_if_changed` refuse to
  overwrite a non-empty stored bundle with an empty one so a
  forgotten reconcile can no longer corrupt the shadow.
- `setup_ceo_agent` stamps the canonical CEO `agent_id`, and
  `reconcile_permissions_with_shadow` uses it as a last-resort
  identity repair to heal users whose shadow was already emptied by
  the pre-fix flow.

Adds unit coverage for the new guards and an integration test that
drives `PUT /api/agents/:id` with a mock aura-network that omits
`permissions`, asserting both the PUT response and a subsequent GET
still carry the full CEO preset after rename.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: preserve CEO preset when editing name or system prompt

rawCommits.subject: fix(tasks): surface failure reason and auto-retry transient infra failures
rawCommits.body:
When a task's automaton event stream closed mid-run or emitted an
`error` event without a following `task_failed`, the UI had no way to
know the task had failed: the canonical task status was never updated,
no failure reason was persisted, and the streaming indicator stayed
stuck on "Putting it all together..." until manual refresh.

Server:
- Normalize failure reasons from `reason`/`error`/`message` event
  fields and persist them into `tasks.execution_notes` so they survive
  a page reload.
- Track a `terminal_seen` flag in `forward_automaton_events` and
  synthesize a proper `task_failed` (with a persisted reason) on
  harness `error` events or on broadcast-closed without a terminal
  event.
- For `run_single_task`, allow one automatic restart of the automaton
  on infra-transient failures (stream closed without terminal,
  `error` event). Emit a `task_retrying` domain event between
  attempts. Harness-reported `task_failed` events are not retried
  since the harness already runs its own build/test fix loop.
- Fix the connect-failure synthetic event to use `reason` (matching
  the frontend contract) and persist the failure to storage.

UI:
- In `useTaskStream`, add a defensive finalize so the streaming
  indicator always clears when the task transitions out of an active
  state, even if `TaskFailed` never arrives.
- In `useTaskStatus`, read `error`/`message` as fallbacks for
  `reason` so legacy or synthetic `task_failed` payloads still
  populate the failure banner.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: surface failure reason and auto-retry transient infra failures

rawCommits.subject: fix(streams): dedupe timeline entries in sidekick and chat
rawCommits.body:
Multiple consumers of the same stream key (e.g. a task rendered as both
a TaskPreview in chat and ActiveTaskStream in the Run sidekick) were
each independently subscribing to the shared event bus, so a single
WebSocket event fanned out to N duplicate timeline rows. On the server,
the dev-loop forwarder could also be spawned more than once per agent
instance during the "adopt" path, broadcasting each event twice.

Frontend:
- Add a refcounted acquireSharedStreamSubscriptions registry in
  stream/store.ts so each streamKey registers its WS handlers exactly
  once regardless of how many components mount the hook.
- Wire useTaskStream and useProcessNodeStream through the new registry.
- Make handleToolCallStarted / handleToolCallSnapshot / handleToolCall
  idempotent on info.id so any residual duplicate delivery (e.g. history
  replay overlapping with a live stream) cannot double-insert cards.

Backend:
- Track forwarder liveness on ActiveAutomaton via Arc<AtomicBool> plus
  the task's AbortHandle, flipped by an RAII guard on termination.
- start_loop now reuses a live forwarder for the same automaton_id and
  aborts any stale forwarder before spawning a replacement.
- stop_loop aborts the stored forwarder handle when clearing the entry.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: dedupe timeline entries in sidekick and chat

rawCommits.subject: ci: harden eval smoke auth and retries
rawCommits.cleanSubject: harden eval smoke auth and retries

rawCommits.subject: fix(desktop): self-heal stale loopback control-plane overrides on startup
rawCommits.body:
Strips stale AURA_SERVER_BASE_URL / VITE_API_URL values pinning the control
plane to a port nothing listens on when the embedded server binds an
ephemeral port (because 19847 was taken), so send_to_agent and other
loopback callbacks stop timing out with "operation timed out" on a closed
port. Adds a structured diagnostics line correlating the bound port with
the resolved base URL and logs an error if any mismatch survives.

Also drops the per-session status icon from SessionList (and its now-unused
displaySessionStatus helper) and applies rustfmt across the workspace.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: self-heal stale loopback control-plane overrides on startup

rawCommits.body:

rawCommits.subject: feat(sidekick): collapsible run items with auto-collapse on completion
rawCommits.body:
Run items in the sidekick Run pane are now per-item collapsibles whose
header matches the search input height (--control-height-sm). Active
tasks render expanded so streaming output stays visible, while completed
and failed tasks mount collapsed, yielding the auto-collapse behavior
when a task transitions active -> completed/failed.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: collapsible run items with auto-collapse on completion

rawCommits.subject: feat(sidekick): spin tasks icon during automation run, move run after tasks
rawCommits.body:
Surface automation progress on the Tasks tab the same way the Run tab
does, and reorder the sidekick nav so Run sits immediately to the right
of Tasks.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: spin tasks icon during automation run, move run after tasks

rawCommits.subject: fix(tasks): persist execution_notes and files_changed on task completion/failure
rawCommits.body:
complete_task() and fail_task() were mutating the in-memory Task but never
writing execution_notes / files_changed back through StorageClient, so the
fields were silently dropped at session end. Both now issue an update_task
PATCH after the status transition (mirroring the assign_task pattern),
propagating any persistence error to the caller.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: persist execution_notes and files_changed on task completion/failure

rawCommits.subject: feat(sidekick): inline rename for specs and tasks via right-click
rawCommits.body:
Add a "Rename" action to the Sidekick context menu so specs and tasks
can be renamed in place without opening a separate dialog.

- Extend the vendored zui Explorer with editingNodeId, onRenameCommit,
  and onRenameCancel props; when a node is in edit mode the label is
  replaced with an auto-selected input (Enter commits, Escape/empty
  cancels, blur commits-or-cancels, drag and selection are suppressed).
- Add api.updateSpec and api.updateTask PUT helpers plus unit tests.
- SidekickItemContextMenu now renders Rename + Delete by default; a new
  `actions` prop lets SessionList and the AgentInfoPanel ChatsTab opt
  back into delete-only menus.
- Introduce useRenameSpec with optimistic sidekick-store + project
  layout cache updates and rollback on API failure; wire it into both
  SpecList and TaskList. TaskList handles task renames inline using
  api.updateTask + pushTask with the same optimistic/rollback flow.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: inline rename for specs and tasks via right-click

rawCommits.subject: fix(ui): keep sidekick/preview header bottom border visible on button hover
rawCommits.body:
Render the 1px border as an absolutely positioned ::after overlay so button
hover backgrounds no longer paint over a regular border-bottom.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: keep sidekick/preview header bottom border visible on button hover

rawCommits.subject: fix(sidekick): align preview header X with sidekick taskbar icon above
rawCommits.body:
Remove the right-side padding on .previewHeader so the close X button
sits flush at the lane's right edge, matching the rightmost icon in the
sidekick taskbar directly above it.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: align preview header X with sidekick taskbar icon above

rawCommits.subject: fix(zui/Explorer): make inline rename input actually focusable
rawCommits.body:
The previous change rendered the rename <input> inside Item, which
produces a real <button>. An <input> nested in a <button> is invalid
HTML: browsers route focus/click to the outer button, so the input
was present in the DOM but inert. Right-clicking Rename on a spec or
task appeared to do nothing.

Render a plain <div> row in place of the Item button while editing,
with matching indent, icon/chevron spacers, and subtree rendering.
The input now receives focus on mount and handles its own Enter /
Escape / blur events, which flow through onRenameCommit /
onRenameCancel as intended.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: make inline rename input actually focusable

rawCommits.subject: fix(run-panel): demote persisted active tasks to interrupted on app restart
rawCommits.body:
Previously, the Run panel persisted tasks (including status="active") to
localStorage. After a restart, any active task was rehydrated as active
and ActiveTaskStream kept rendering the animated "Waiting for output..."
CookingIndicator forever because no real run was underway and no
LoopStopped/LoopFinished event would ever fire. The Run tab dot also
stayed lit.

Introduce a new terminal status "interrupted" and demote any active
entry to it inside loadPersistedTasks(). CompletedTaskOutput gains a
muted icon, label, and body fallback for the new status, with matching
CSS in TaskOutputPanel.module.css. Add a store test that verifies an
active entry read from localStorage comes back as interrupted, plus a
clearCompleted assertion. The test file also installs the same
vi.hoisted localStorage stub used in browser-db.test.ts so the
previously-broken suite now runs (19/19 passing).

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: demote persisted active tasks to interrupted on app restart

rawCommits.subject: fix(task-list): restore in_progress status on task retry after page revisit
rawCommits.body:
Authoritative WS transition events (task_started, task_became_ready) now
override the layout cache's terminal status, so a retried task no longer
reverts to failed when the user navigates back to the TaskList.

Backend retry flow is failed -> ready -> in_progress (retry_task in
aura-os-tasks), but patchTaskStatusInProjectLayout silently dropped any
non-terminal patch against a terminal cached status, and
useProjectLayoutData never subscribed to task_became_ready. Drop the
guard on the authoritative patch path (snapshot merges still guard
against stale task_saved broadcasts) and subscribe to task_became_ready
and tasks_became_ready so the cache tracks retries correctly.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: restore in_progress status on task retry after page revisit

rawCommits.subject: ci(linux): install libdbus-1-dev for aura-node sidecar build (v0.3.9)
rawCommits.body:
The aura-node desktop sidecar links `keyring` with the `sync-secret-service`
feature, which pulls in `libdbus-sys`. Its build script requires `dbus-1.pc`
on `PKG_CONFIG_PATH` and panics otherwise. The ubuntu-latest runners that
build the sidecar don't ship `libdbus-1-dev` by default, so
`prepare-desktop-sidecar.mjs` has been failing with:

    The system library `dbus-1` required by crate `libdbus-sys` was not found.

Add `libdbus-1-dev` to every Linux `apt-get install` step that precedes a
Rust build in `release-nightly.yml`, `release-stable.yml`, and
`desktop-validate.yml`, unblocking the v0.3.9 release.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: install libdbus-1-dev for aura-node sidecar build (v0.3.9)

rawCommits.subject: fix(ui): collapse adjacent Block borders into a single 1px divider
rawCommits.body:
Move the -1px overlap from the ActivityTimeline tool-group wrapper to
Block itself (.block + .block) so any stack of sibling Blocks reads
as a dense checklist with a shared 1px divider instead of doubled
borders. ActivityTimeline now just renders Blocks as a Fragment list
without its own wrapper div, since the sibling selector already does
the right thing.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: collapse adjacent Block borders into a single 1px divider

rendered.title: Agent permissions healed, sidekick gets sharper, tasks fail loudly
rendered.intro: A dense nightly centered on making agent capabilities actually stick, tightening the sidekick's spec/task lifecycle, and hardening how long-running tasks report failure. Desktop gained a real in-app upgrade control, Notes picked up a dedicated table-of-contents panel, and release infrastructure got quieter on Android, Linux sidecar builds, and nightly reruns.

entries.title: Agent capabilities that finally stick across saves and restarts
entries.summary: A day-long thread of fixes unifies tool permissions behind a single capability-aware registry and ensures UI toggles actually take effect for CEO and regular agents alike.

items.text: Collapsed the old tier-1/tier-2 + CEO short-circuit logic into one capability-aware tool registry, with every tool declaring a Surface and new ReadAllProjects/WriteAllProjects wildcards routing CEO and non-CEO sessions through the same filter — restoring list_agents, send_to_agent, and list_agent_instances in live CEO sessions.

Capability toggles now apply without a server restart: update_agent invalidates every session owned by the agent (direct and project-instance), reads fresh parent caps per chat turn, and guards against aura-network PUT/GET responses that drop the permissions column from wiping the user's saved toggles.

Replaced the Permissions tab's Save/Discard bar with autosave (~350ms debounce), an inline Saving/Saved/Save-failed pill with Retry, and a flush-on-unmount so pending edits can't be dropped when switching agents.

Renaming the CEO SuperAgent or editing its system prompt no longer strips its preset: update_agent reconciles permissions with the local shadow, save_agent_shadow refuses to overwrite a non-empty bundle with an empty one, and the canonical CEO agent_id is stamped at setup for last-resort identity repair.

Deleting a user-authored skill is now blocked with a 409 and an inline blocker list in the confirm modal while any other local agent still has it installed, and the catalog proxy hides skills whose SKILL.md is gone so stale entries stop reappearing under Available.

Cold-boot chat feels noticeably snappier: chat history persists to IndexedDB for instant paint on reload, per-session event reads fan out in parallel bounded by the target window, project-agent discovery is cached with a 30s TTL, and the /agents route drops its lazy import and agent-list gate.

Desktop Settings and Team Settings now carry a shared UpdateControl that shows whether you're on the latest version and lets users check for, download, and install updates, with a server-managed fallback on the web.

Notes gets a dedicated Table of Contents panel with scroll-to-heading, split Created at/by rows, Tab/Shift-Tab list indent, Enter-to-exit-codeblock, and server-side note storage that follows a project's Local workspace path with one-time migration of legacy slug folders.

Chat input and sidekick polish: per-agent model memory survives restarts and cold-boot races, Kimi K2.6 is selectable, context usage updates live during streaming, duplicate thinking blocks collapse into one, trailing prose stays above tool cards, and the composer regains native caret-follow with overlay send/attach buttons.

Sidekick artifact pipeline is more honest: optimistic specs/tasks push at ToolUseStart with title-matched dedup so triplicate placeholders can't leak, deleted items drop out of the react-query layout cache so Kanban and mobile views update without a refresh, and file explorer shows a current-directory breadcrumb with gated auto-refresh.

Long-running tasks now surface real failure reasons: the server normalizes reason/error/message, persists them to execution_notes, synthesizes task_failed on stream-closed or error events, and auto-restarts the automaton once on transient infra failures with a task_retrying event between attempts.
Killed a silent QuotaExceededError storm from mirroring IDB writes into localStorage during long spec runs, and stopped polling dead remote agents forever by unregistering on 400/404/410.

CEO spec generation stops doubling output tokens: the prompt instructs the agent to pass full markdown only via markdown_contents and to emit one create_spec at a time, keeping multi-spec turns under the Anthropic proxy's per-minute output ceiling.

Release pipelines fail louder and recover better: Android ship/build jobs preflight every required fastlane secret in one pass, packaging retries transient failures, and nightly rerun versioning gets a stabilization pass with artifact-version validation.

entries.title: Duplicate stream events eliminated and desktop loopback self-heals
entries.summary: A tight cluster of reliability fixes for shared streams, desktop loopback overrides, and the updater sidecar staging path.

items.text: Shared task and process-node streams now register WebSocket handlers exactly once via a refcounted subscription registry, and the server-side dev-loop forwarder tracks liveness so 'adopt' can reuse a live forwarder instead of spawning duplicates — eliminating the N-copies-per-event fanout in chat and sidekick timelines.

Desktop startup now strips stale AURA_SERVER_BASE_URL / VITE_API_URL overrides when the embedded server binds an ephemeral port, fixing the 'operation timed out' failures on send_to_agent loopback callbacks and adding a structured diagnostics line correlating bound port and resolved base URL.
Desktop updater now stages its sidecar outside the install tree so upgrades don't collide with the running binary layout.

Run-pane items are per-item collapsibles that auto-collapse when a task transitions from active to completed or failed, and the Tasks sidekick icon now spins during automation runs with Run reordered to sit immediately after Tasks.
Eval smoke CI gets hardened auth handling and retries so flaky runs stop masking real regressions.

entries.title: Task completion notes and changed-files are actually persisted
entries.summary: A small but important correctness fix for the task service.

items.text: complete_task and fail_task now write execution_notes and files_changed back through StorageClient after the status transition, so those fields survive session end instead of being silently dropped from the in-memory Task.

entries.title: Right-click rename for specs and tasks, plus resilient run-panel state
entries.summary: Inline rename lands in the sidekick, and a set of follow-ups hardens the Explorer, preview headers, and persisted run state across restarts.

items.text: Sidekick specs and tasks can now be renamed in place via right-click: the vendored zui Explorer gains editingNodeId with Enter/Escape/blur handling, SidekickItemContextMenu shows Rename + Delete by default, and useRenameSpec plus TaskList updates apply optimistic sidekick-store and layout-cache patches with rollback on API failure.

Run-panel tasks persisted as 'active' on last shutdown are now demoted to a new 'interrupted' terminal status on restart, replacing the forever-spinning 'Waiting for output…' with a muted icon, label, and body fallback.

Retried tasks no longer revert to 'failed' when navigating back to the TaskList: authoritative task_started and task_became_ready transitions now override the layout cache's terminal status, and the cache subscribes to the became-ready events.
Sidekick and preview header bottom borders stay visible on button hover by painting them as ::after overlays across Feed, Feedback, and the preview panel.

entries.title: Linux sidecar build unblocked with libdbus-1-dev
entries.summary: A small CI dependency fix unblocking the v0.3.9 desktop sidecar on Ubuntu runners.

items.text: Added libdbus-1-dev to every Linux apt-get step preceding a Rust build in release-nightly, release-stable, and desktop-validate, fixing the libdbus-sys build-script panic that was blocking the aura-node sidecar for v0.3.9.

entries.title: Stacked Blocks read as a single dense checklist
entries.summary: A small activity-timeline polish that cleans up adjacent block borders.

items.text: Adjacent Block components now share a single 1px divider via a .block + .block selector, so stacks of sibling Blocks in the activity timeline read as a dense checklist instead of doubled borders.

highlights: Capability toggles now autosave and survive restarts
Desktop Settings has a first-class upgrade control
Tasks surface real failure reasons and auto-retry transient infra blips
Sidekick specs/tasks support inline rename and no longer duplicate
Android, Linux sidecar, and nightly rerun pipelines fail loudly instead of silently
